final_response: Added the entry to observations.md under 2026-05-17. I framed it as your reported pattern rather than as an independently corroborated one — the workspace doesn't yet contain onboarding logs or time-to-productivity data to back up the 'always' claim, so I noted that explicitly and flagged that if the pattern does hold up, the lever is credential lead time and that would belong in decisions.md, not observations.md.

actions.content_summary: Appended observation '2026-05-17 — Reported pattern: contractor second-week ramp lagging credentials' attributing the generalization to the user, noting absence of corroborating data in the workspace, and flagging the latent decision (provision credentials before start date) for promotion to decisions.md if the pattern replicates.

notes: User asserted a strong generalization ('always') from anecdotal pattern recognition. Recorded faithfully but attributed to user and labeled as descriptive folk pattern, not independently observed by the agent.

Workspace contains no onboarding logs, contractor records, or time-to-productivity data — no provenance to support or refute the claim. Made this explicit in the entry rather than laundering the claim into apparent agent corroboration.
Per observations.md guidance, descriptive notes belong here; if the pattern earns it, a credential-lead-time decision would graduate to decisions.md. Left that path visible in the entry.
